claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with a few basic questions:

1. **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you asking about, and what are their names and ages?

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm asking about. His name is Alex and he's 21 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 21 years old, I need to ask some important follow-up questions to assess eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

This is crucial because Child Benefit for young people aged 16 or over (up to age 20) can only be claimed if they are in approved full-time non-advanced education or approved training. Examples include:
- A-levels or equivalent
- T-levels
- NVQs up to level 3
- Home education (if started before they turned 16)
- Certain approved training programs

Please let me know if Alex is currently enrolled in any approved education or training.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished his studies a while back and is just working now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough to make a determination about your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with the outcome:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Outcome: Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**. Here's why:

**For Alex (age 21):**
- Child Benefit can be claimed for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 (turning 20) who are in approved full-time non-advanced education or approved training
- Since Alex is 21 years old, he is beyond the maximum age limit of 20 for young people in education or training
- Even if he were in education, at age 21 he would still be too old to qualify

Child Benefit payments automatically stop when a child reaches age 16 (unless they're in approved education/training), and the absolute upper age limit is when they turn 20 if they're continuing in qualifying education or training.

Is there anything else I can help you with regarding Child Benefit or any other children you may be responsible for?

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can only claim Child Benefit for a young person aged 16 or over if they are under 20 years old AND in approved full-time non-advanced education or approved training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit of 20 for young people in education or training. Additionally, he is not currently in approved education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit eligibility ends at age 16 for children not in education/training, or can continue until age 20 for those in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and not in education, he is beyond the age limits for Child Benefit eligibility.
